prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ETS', seasonality_champion='ETS', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: ETS dominates both trend tracking (lowest slope error 0.2267) and seasonality capture (highest correlation 0.7496), while ONLY_DWT_catboost achieves the best overall RMSE. Significant model disagreement exists with rmse_spread_ratio=0.4637 and extreme seasonality variance (0.9103), plus distinct early/late horizon specialists. These patterns strongly support DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths across models.
prompts.skeptic:

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ETS",
  "seasonality_champion": "ETS",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_DWT_catboost",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "CWT_rf", "late": "NaiveSeasonal"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_DWT_catboost", "FT_catboost", "FT_rf", "DWT_rf", "THETA", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "CWT_svr", "CWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "ONLY_FT_svr", "NaiveMovingAverage", "CWT_catboost", "rf", "catboost", "DWT_catboost"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.4637,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "ETS dominates both trend tracking (lowest slope error 0.2267) and seasonality capture (highest correlation 0.7496), while ONLY_DWT_catboost achieves the best overall RMSE. Significant model disagreement exists with rmse_spread_ratio=0.4637 and extreme seasonality variance (0.9103), plus distinct early/late horizon specialists. These patterns strongly support DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths across models."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "balanced",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates high model heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection. RMSE spread between best (FT_catboost: 17888) and worst (DWT_svr: 24091) is ~38%, favoring top-k over full mean. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality_variance=0.9103 and model disagreement. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 provides stability while shrinkage=0.25 prevents overfitting. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific specialists."
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: ETS dominates both trend tracking (lowest slope error 0.2267) and seasonality capture (highest correlation 0.7496), while ONLY_DWT_catboost achieves the best overall RMSE. Significant model disagreement exists with rmse_spread_ratio=0.4637 and extreme seasonality variance (0.9103), plus distinct early/late horizon specialists. These patterns strongly support DBA combination to leverage complementary strengths across models.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates high model heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection. RMSE spread between best (FT_catboost: 17888) and worst (DWT_svr: 24091) is ~38%, favoring top-k over full mean. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality_variance=0.9103 and model disagreement. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 provides stability while shrinkage=0.25 prevents overfitting. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific specialists.
